title: $result
text: $result Return the result of certain ProcScript commands and tree widget events. $result $result = Expression Return Values The $result function is set by many ProcScript statements and by tree widget events. For the values $result can contain, see the documentation for the individual ProcScript statements. Use Use in Form, Report, and Service components. $result = Expression is allowed in Form components, but not in self-contained Report and Service components. Description $result receives the result of several ProcScript command and tree widget events. For example: First column of the last selected occurrence when a SELECT statement is included in an sql statement. Status of comparison made with the compare statement. Status of actions performed with $ude In the debugger, $result can be accessed directly or as variable $101 . Related Links = (compute) Assigns the value of an expression to a field, variable, or function. addmonths Adds the specified number of months to the date, storing the result of the calculation in $result. filebox Displays a file selection box. length (deprecated) Return the number of characters in the specified string. print Activate printing. sql Pass a DML statement to the specified DBMS path. Status Determines whether the status data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence modification state. It can be set or inspected using $occstatus. Valid values are True or False (default). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
